data.title: UBS Names New Chief Operating Officer in Executive Team Reshuffle -- Update
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud


UBS Group named Beatriz Martin, head of noncore assets, new chief operating officer in a reshuffle of its executive team as the bank nominated compliance chief Markus Ronner as vice chairman of its board.

The Swiss bank said Friday that Lukas Gaehwiler, vice chairman of its board since 2022, wouldn't stand for re-election at next year's annual general meeting as he decided to retire.

UBS proposed Ronner, currently the group's chief compliance and governance officer, to succeed Gaehwiler as board vice chair. It also appointed Michelle Bereaux, chief integration officer, as head compliance and operational risk control, and transferred responsibility for governmental and regulatory affairs under the remit of Todd Tuckner, its finance chief.

The executive shake-up puts Martin in charge of finalizing the integration of Credit Suisse as COO. She will keep her current responsibilities as sustainability lead and head of the group's noncore and legacy business--which houses assets formerly owned by Credit Suisse it wants to exit--as well as president for the Europe, Middle East and Africa region and chief executive for the U.K., UBS said.

Bereaux, who was named chief group integration officer in 2023 and led the completion of client migrations of Switzerland and part of the work in the bank's home country, will relinquish her responsibilities to assume her new role, UBS said.

The bank said Gaehwiler, who is retiring after a 45-year career in the financial sector, played a key role in the integration of Credit Suisse, having served as its final chair.

"With these changes, UBS is ideally positioned to successfully complete the integration, focus on growth opportunities and continue to deliver in the best interest of all our key stakeholders," UBS Chief Executive Sergio Ermotti said.

UBS said it remains on track to complete the Credit Suisse integration by the end of 2026.


Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0239ET

data.title: ASEAN to host Trump at summit as US and China seek to avert trade war escalation
Vincent Rouget will take over as head of URW in early 2026
data.description:
Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW) announces that Vincent Rouget, currently Chief Strategy and Investment Officer, who serves as Chief Operating Officer for Europe, has been appointed Chairman of the Management Board effective January 1, 2026.

This decision reflects the real estate group's outlook and the positive trajectory set out in the 2025-28 roadmap established by Jean-Marie Tritant, Chairman of the Management Board since 2021, who will oversee the transition until the end of the year.

In addition, URW announces the acquisition of a 25% stake in St James Quarter, a mixed-use destination in Edinburgh combining 110 retail, leisure and dining units across 80,300 sq m, with 152 high-end apartments.

As part of this new joint venture partnership with majority shareholder APG, which will retain a 75% stake, URW will manage the asset, which will be renamed Westfield in 2026.

data.title: Eni Increases Buyback, Cost-Savings Target
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker


Eni increased its full-year buyback commitment after raising its cost-savings target, as third-quarter earnings rose with higher production offsetting weaker oil prices.

The Italian oil-and-gas major said Friday that it will now buy back 1.8 billion euros ($2.09 billion) of shares over the full year, a 300 million euro increase on its previous commitment as it benefits from a higher free cash flow and cash-saving initiatives.

Eni also increased its full-year cash flow before working capital adjustments guidance to 12 billion euros from 11.5 billion euros, and said that it will target around 4 billion euros in costs savings, up from a previous target of 3 billion euros.

The guidance upgrades came as Eni's earnings rose and beat expectations. Net profit rose to 803 million euros from 543 million euros in the prior quarter. Adjusted net profit increased to 1.25 billion euros, rising from 1.13 billion euros and beating the 1.02 billion euros analysts had expected according to a company-compiled consensus.

The company said crude oil prices declined 14% over the quarter but this was partially offset by a rise in production to 1.76 million barrels of oil equivalent a day. Eni increased its oil-and-gas production guidance to between 1.71 million and 1.72 million barrels of oil equivalent a day.


Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0237ET

data.title: Stallion India to Start Construction at New Manufacturing Unit in Bhilwara, India; Shares Down 5%
Sanofi increases EPS by 13% at CGU in Q3
data.description:
Sanofi has reported Q3 2025 operating EPS of €2.91, up 7% (+13.2% at constant exchange rates or CCE), "supported by disciplined cost management, a focus on R&D and operational efficiency."

Its sales increased by 2.3% to €12.43bn (+7% at CGU). Sales of new pharmaceutical launches climbed 57.1% to €1bn, and sales of Dupixent rose 26.2% to €4.2bn, but vaccine sales declined 7.8% to €3.4bn.

As expected, sales of influenza vaccines declined due to increased price competition and lower vaccination rates, management said.

Sanofi confirms that it expects a strong rebound in EPS from its activities in 2025, with growth at the low end of the double-digit range (excluding share buybacks), and anticipates high single-digit growth in sales at constant currency.

Hexagon Swings to Net Loss in Q3
Hexagon's adjusted operating profit higher than expected (update)
data.description:
(addition in full news item)

Measurement technology company Hexagon reports sales in line with expectations for the third quarter.
Adjusted operating profit was better than expected.

"Organic growth amounted to 4 percent, driven mainly by another strong quarter in Autonomous Solutions and good demand in segments such as aerospace and defense, machine control, and electronics. The operating margin improved sequentially but remains below our target. Cash generation was seasonally strong. The potential spin-off of Octave is proceeding according to plan and is expected to be completed during the first half of
2026," says CEO Anders Svensson.

Sales rose 0.3 percent to EUR 1,303.8 million (1,299.8). The outcome can be compared with Bloomberg's analyst consensus of 1,317.

Adjusted operating profit amounted to EUR 349.3 million (376.6), compared with an expected EUR 336 million, with an adjusted operating margin of 26.8 percent (29.0).

Operating profit amounted to EUR -12.9 million (333.4). Adjustments are included at EUR -362.2 million (-42.2).

Operating cash flow before non-recurring items increased to EUR 177.8 million (165.9).

Cash generation amounted to 77 percent (70).

Recurring revenue increased organically by 6 percent and amounted to EUR 564.2 million (564.9).

"Signals from our markets point to an unchanged climate in the fourth quarter of 2025," Anders Svensson continues.

Hexagon announced in its Q2 report that a cost-saving program had been initiated.

"In our review of the cost base, we identified potential savings of EUR 110 million on an annual basis, with a one-time cost of EUR 113 million. These savings, which cover both Hexagon and Octave, will gradually begin to positively impact the cost base from the fourth quarter of 2025 and reach full effect towards the end of 2026," says Svensson.

He adds.

"We have also identified investments in research and development and related inventories totaling €186 million that no longer correspond to customer or market needs. We have therefore decided to write down these items."

Hexagon, MEURQ3-2025 ConsensusChange from consensusQ3-2024 Change
Net sales 1,303.8 1,317 - 1,299.8 0.3
Adjusted operating profit 349.3 336 4.0 376.6 -7.2
Adjusted operating margin 26.8 25.5 29.0
Consensus data from Bloomberg

Intel shares up nearly 10% after third quarter profit beat
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) -Intel shares rose nearly 10% in Frankfurt on Friday, a day after the company beat expectations for its third quarter profit helped by dramatic cost cutting measures.

The results were the company's first earnings announcement after multibillion-dollar investments from Nvidia and Japan's SoftBank as well as an unprecedented U.S. government stake, with investors anticipating a major cash boost.

Its shares rose around 7% in U.S. after hours trading on Thursday, and rose 9.7% in Frankfurt on Friday.

(Reporting by Alun John, editing by Joice Alves)

Folksam to Reduce Stake in Swedbank
data.description:
Insurance company Folksamgruppen intends to reduce its stake in Swedbank from the current 7 percent to approximately 5.5 percent. The adjustment will take place over time and at a pace dictated by market conditions, Folksam announced in a press release.

"We continue to take a long-term view of our holding in Swedbank, and even after this adjustment, we will remain the bank's second largest shareholder," said Marcus Blomberg, Head of Asset Management and Sustainability at Folksamgruppen.

The decision was made to decrease risk exposure, which has increased in line with the positive development of Swedbank's share price, according to Folksam, which has been a major shareholder in Swedbank since 2008.

data.title: Dian Tou Energy's Q3 Profit Slips 9%, Revenue Rises 3%
Eni Reports EUR 1.25 Billion Adjusted Net Profit in Q3, Raises Buyback Program
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Eni Spa announced on Friday that it recorded an adjusted net profit of EUR 1.25 billion in the third quarter, down 2% from the same period in 2024, when it stood at EUR 1.27 billion.

The pro-forma adjusted EBIT amounted to EUR 3.00 billion, marking a 12% decrease from EUR 3.40 billion a year earlier. Adjusted pre-tax profit also declined by 14% year-on-year in the third quarter to EUR 2.27 billion from EUR 2.66 billion.

Hydrocarbon production increased by 6% year-on-year, reaching 1.76 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, up from 1.66 million, thanks to the launch of new projects and strong operational performance.

The Exploration & Production segment posted an adjusted EBIT of EUR 2.64 billion, down 19% year-on-year, while the Global Gas & LNG Portfolio and Power division saw a 21% improvement to EUR 346 million. Enilive and Plenitude contributed EUR 331 million, up 8%, while the Refining business returned to profit at EUR 140 million.

Installed renewable capacity reached 4.8 GW, a 55% increase year-on-year, and net financial debt was reduced to EUR 9.9 billion, with pre-lease leverage at 19%.

Adjusted operating cash flow rose to EUR 3.3 billion, up 14% from the previous year, against organic investments of EUR 2 billion.

Over the nine-month period, net profit climbed to EUR 2.52 billion from EUR 2.39 billion in the first nine months of 2024, with the adjusted figure down 13% to EUR 3.79 billion from EUR 4.37 billion. Adjusted pre-tax profit for the nine months fell to EUR 7.22 billion from EUR 9.20 billion, and adjusted EBIT dropped 19% to EUR 9.36 billion from EUR 11.62 billion.

Eni also increased its buyback program for this year to EUR 1.8 billion, up EUR 300 million from the previous forecast, and confirmed its annual dividend at EUR 1.05 per share, with the second tranche of EUR 0.26 to be paid on November 26, as announced yesterday.

For the full year, the company expects operating cash flow of EUR 12 billion, up from the initially expected EUR 11.5 billion, average production of 1.71-1.72 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, and installed renewable capacity at year-end of 5.5 GW. Proforma leverage is expected to range between 0.15 and 0.18.

Investments are forecast at EUR 8.5 billion, down from the initial figure of EUR 9 billion, and EBIT is expected to be EUR 1 billion higher than previously guided, thanks to portfolio optimizations.

Claudio Descalzi, Eni's CEO, commented: "Third-quarter results are excellent, and all the main operational, economic, and financial variables have exceeded expectations. Production of 1.76 million barrels per day is growing strongly and allows us to raise annual guidance to 1.72 million barrels per day, confirming the acceleration trend set to continue in the coming months thanks to new fields under development in Congo, UAE, Qatar, and Libya, as well as the launch of the business combination in Indonesia and Malaysia, which will become one of the leading LNG market players in Asia."

"The value creation of our businesses continues with proceeds from the sale of 30% of the Baleine field in Ivory Coast, following our established dual exploration model, and with progress on the sale of a 20% stake in Plenitude to the Ares fund, for which all conditions precedent have been fulfilled. With this operation, the two businesses, Enilive and Plenitude, have generated proceeds of approximately EUR 6.5 billion over the past two years."

"Essentially, the third quarter demonstrates how all the key elements of our strategy are successfully advancing in parallel: we are achieving competitive growth in all our core businesses; in upstream, we are launching new projects while securing new opportunities through our industry-leading exploration and technological know-how; and we are opening up new opportunities in the energy transition space. At the same time, we are continuously creating more value in terms of risk/return management through our dual exploration model and satellite strategy, enabling us to reduce debt and share value creation with our shareholders," the CEO added.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

Hexagon's Adjusted Operating Profit Surpasses Expectations
data.description:
Measurement technology company Hexagon reported third-quarter revenue in line with expectations, while its adjusted operating profit exceeded forecasts.

Revenue rose by 0.3 percent to EUR1,303.8 million (EUR1,299.8 million in Q3 2024), compared to a Bloomberg analyst consensus of EUR1,317 million.

The adjusted operating profit amounted to EUR349.3 million (EUR376.6 million in Q3 2024), surpassing the expected EUR336 million. The adjusted operating margin stood at 26.8 percent (29.0 percent).

Adjusted Operating Margin 26.8% 25.5% 29.0%
Consensus data from Bloomberg

data.title: Zhejiang Supor's Q3 Profit Falls 13%, Revenue Slip 2%
Accor confirms outlook for FY 2025
data.description:
The hotels group recorded revenue of €1,369m for Q3 2025, up 0.1% at constant exchange rates compared to Q3 2024.

This increase breaks down into a 1.1% decline at constant exchange rates for the Premium, Midscale and Economy division and a 0.2% increase at constant exchange rates for the Luxury & Lifestyle division.

The Premium, Midscale and Economy (PM&E) division posted a 1.1% decline in RevPAR compared to Q3 2024.

The Luxury & Lifestyle (L&L) division posted a 5.0% increase in RevPAR compared to Q3 2024.

During Q3 2025, Accor opened 77 hotels, representing 11,200 rooms, for a net growth of 2.5% over the last 12 months. At the end of September 2025, the Group had a portfolio of 859,830 rooms (5,760 hotels) and a pipeline of over 250,000 rooms (1,453 hotels).

Accor confirms its outlook for FY 2025, targeting RevPAR growth of between 3% and 4% and network growth of around 3.5%.
